observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the:
(a) accountable officers to improve further their compliance on the reporting/ liquidation of cash advances; and
(b) City Accountant, in collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that would require accountable officers to settle or liquidate advances immediately; and
(c) if conditions and circumstances on writing-off the accounts would be present, direct the City Accountant and the Legal officer to initiate the procedures for filing a request for authority to write-off dormant accounts under COA Circular 2016-005.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
Submission of accounts to the Audit Team was improved, though there are still unliquidated cash advances as at year-end.

(b) Not Implemented
The City Accountant has initiated sending demand letters to the employees with outstanding cash advance as of audit recommendation, the balance of cash advance as of year-end increased by ₱1,001,421.42 compared to previous year’s balance.

(c) Not Implemented
Management is still in the process of collating all the supporting documents for Request for Write-off of dormant accounts, but no formal request was submitted during the year. Moreover, the existing regulations on cash advances should be observed at all times to avoid the state of dormancy. There are still CAs granted to individuals with unliquidated CA, as per monitoring of Schedule of CAs submitted by the City Accountant.
observations.reason: There are still CAs granted to individuals with unliquidated CA, as per monitoring of Schedule of CAs submitted by the City Accountant. Management is still in the process of collating all the supporting documents for Request for Write-off of dormant accounts, but no formal request was submitted during the year.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the:
(a) City Accountant to coordinate with the City Treasurer, reconcile their records of RPT/ SET Receivables and thereafter, conduct regular reconciliation exercise to support the integrity of the accounts; and
(b) City Treasurer to introduce collection strategy/ies that would significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the City; and institute remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local Government Code of 1991.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Not Implemented
Both the City Accountant and the City Treasurer significantly reduced the variance in their records, from ₱8,796,134.15 to (₱94,732.20), particularly in the SEF Tax Receivable account.

(b) Not Implemented
The City Treasurer consistently posted notices of delinquencies in publicly accessible locations. House campaign, sending of demand letters were also conducted which already improved the collection.
observations.reason: Despite posting notices and demand letters and some improvement in collection, delinquent balances continued to accumulate; full implementation of stronger collection strategies and legal remedies remains outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) Inventory Committee in coordination with CGSO to exert best effort to complete the physical count of all inventory items of the City Government, prepare the RPCSP and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and
(b) CGSO to strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated forms, registries and reports pertaining to inventories.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Not Implemented
The Inventory Committee was able to physically count in the supply or property unit. Only those properties physically counted in the supply or property unit should have been physically counted and included in the RPCSP. Those already issued were just monitored in the respective records.

(b) Not Implemented
The CGSO and City Accountant still have not fully complied with the required maintenance of forms, registries and reports pertaining to inventories.
observations.reason: There are still inventory items not covered by the proper RPCSP and forms/registries remain incomplete; monitoring and reconciliation remain outstanding.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require and instruct the:
(a) Inventory Committee and CGSO to: (i) conduct complete physical inventory of all PPE of the City on or before January 31 of each year, conclude said inventory count and immediately submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records; and
(b) City Accountant and the CGSO to: (i) complete the necessary procedures enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the missing/unlocated PPE items and unidentifiable balances; (ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to render a reliable PPE information; and
(c) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines and procedures.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Not Implemented
Inventory Committee and CGSO still conducted physical count in the PPE as of January 31, 2025, and the RPCPPE submitted to the Audit Team as of December 31, 2024 is still incomplete.

(a.ii) Not Implemented
Inventory report/ RPCPPE as of December 31, 2024 is still not reconciled with the accounting records, having a net variance of ₱88,963,956.43.

(b.i) Not Implemented
The City Accountant and the CGSO have not yet completed the necessary procedures under COA Circular No. 2020-006 but have undertaken follow-up actions.

(b.ii) Not Implemented
Consideration of one-time cleansing of PPE accounts pending.

(c) Implemented
Some unserviceable PPEs were conducted auction for some unserviceable PPEs during CY 2024. Some unserviceable PPEs that were not included in the 2024 auction still remain and are scheduled for auction in CY 2025.
observations.reason: RPCPPE as of December 31, 2024 is still not reconciled with the accounting records resulting in a net variance. Management completed physical count after Exit Conference; some procedures remain pending and further actions required for one-time cleansing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City General Services Officer to insure all insurable properties of the City with the GSIS and to coordinate with the City Accountant and the City Treasurer for the timely processing and payment of GSIS insurance premiums in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656.
observations.managementAction:
Not Implemented
Not all insurable properties were insured to the GSIS.
observations.reason: There are still insurable properties that were not insured to the GSIS.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive -
(a) instruct the City Accountant to: (i) properly monitor and initiate thorough review of the composition of the Due to NGAs account and trace the pertinent records and documents/ MOA on the fund transfers from various NGAs; and (ii) effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, if any, to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the books of the agency; and
(b) remind the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to: (i) cause the immediate return of the unutilized and/ or non- moving funds to the respective Source Agencies (NGAs) in observance of Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994; and (ii) consider the adoption of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on requesting for authority to Write-Off of dormant accounts to clear the books of any unaccounted balances.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Not Implemented
The City Accountant reiterated that records of the majority of the accounts recorded under Due to NGAs were not turned over by the previous City Accountants and cannot be located, hindering her from remitting the remaining balance to the SAs due to absence of documentary basis. Nonetheless, the current fund transfers were strictly monitored and any unutilized balances were duly remitted to the SAs.

(b.i) Not Implemented
During the Exit Conference, Management considered reappropriating the balances of dormant accounts to various PPAs instead of remitting it to the BTr.
observations.reason: Records of the majority of the accounts recorded under Due to NGAs were not turned over by previous City Accountants and cannot be located, hindering remittance to SAs. Management considered reappropriating dormant balances instead of remitting to BTr which is not provided in COA Circular No. 94-013.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) CDRRMO in coordination with the CPDO: (i) identify and incorporate under a separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the specific PPAs to be implemented and charged against the unexpended previous years’ LDRRMF; and (ii) carefully monitor the implementation of PPAs for LDRRMF; and
(b) City Accountant to effect the necessary adjustment of ₱5,295,681.80 to reflect the correct balance of the Trust Liabilities-DRRM account as at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Implemented
The CDRRMO identified and incorporated prior years’ unexpended appropriations in the LDRRMFIP and monitored PPAs.

(a.ii) Implemented
CDRRMOs monitoring actions undertaken.

(b) Not Implemented
The balance of Trust Liabilities-DRRMF in the financial statement as stated amounted to ₱30,577,075.19 while the total unexpended LDRRMF amounted to ₱30,478,363.18, resulting in a variance of ₱98,712.01.
observations.reason: A small reconciliation variance of ₱98,712.01 remained between Trust Liabilities-DRRMF and total unexpended LDRRMF.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Engineer and the Local Development Council to:
(a) consider addressing possible problems on projects proposed for funding under the 20% DF during the planning phase to avoid delays during the implementation period;
(b) ensure that the three unimplemented projects with total appropriation of ₱10,000,000.00 will be completed as planned; and
(c) maximize project implementation and utilization of the 20% DF in conformity with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and its implementing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
Addressed issues on planning for project implementation.

(b) Implemented
Management was able to implement the Construction of Multi-Purpose Building/ Hall in Barangay Maligaya. The two PPAs were reprogrammed during the year.

(c) Not Implemented
The City showed marginal progress in the implementation of PPAs charged to 20% DF.
observations.reason: Some PPAs were realigned late in the year; overall utilization and implementation remained marginal for some items.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Mayor instruct the City Accountant to:
(a) require other officials concerned in the processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect prompt submission thereof to her office; and
(b) ensure the submission to the Audit Team of the paid DVs, Trial Balances, Financial Statements with supporting documents, and Bank Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed period.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
The City submitted the CY 2023 transaction documents to the Audit Team.

(b) Not Implemented
There were still delays noted in the submission of CY 2024 accounts.
observations.reason: Delays persisted into CY 2024 due to insufficient manpower, staff capability gaps and transitional staffing issues; Management undertook hiring and system change measures but full compliance pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the CSWMB to:
(a) continue to implement and enforce meaningful and relevant PPAs that will help promote the proper waste management to protect the environment and the health and safety of the population;
(b) strictly enforce the at-source segregation of wastes thru the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”; and
(c) revisit the contract agreement with MCWMC including the provisions on the guaranteed minimum volume of waste per bins and make clarification on the policy implemented by the MCWMC that is not contained in the terms and conditions of the Contract.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
CSWMB continued implementing PPAs to promote proper waste management.

(b) Not Implemented
Some wastes from various households were not segregated; strict enforcement ongoing.

(c) Not Implemented
The Audit Team requested a copy of the latest contract; however, none was received. The CENRO staff mentioned that there was no amendment/change made.
observations.reason: At-source segregation not yet fully enforced across households; contract clarifications with MCWMC pending and contract copy not provided to auditors.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the CBPLO to:
(a) strictly enforce the submission of Audited FS or in its absence, the Sworn Statement of Gross Receipts/ Sales or a copy of ITR from taxpayers to verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as provided by 20.04 (d) of the Revenue Code;
(b) monitor the registration/ renewal of Mayor’s Permit of every business establishment in the City of Palayan; and
(c) make proper coordination with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to amend/ include in the Revenue Code the imposition of penalties/ surcharges for non-registration/renewal of Mayor’s Permit.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
CBPLO enforced submission of required documents where applicable.

(b) Implemented
Monitoring of registration/renewal improved.

(c) Not Implemented
Coordination with Sangguniang Panlungsod for amendments pending.
observations.reason: Amendments to Revenue Code requiring penalties/surcharges for non-renewal require SP action which is pending.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) CEEMO (i) in coordination with the City Treasurer, to consider the proposal of an ordinance to the Sangguniang Panlunsod covering the imposition or fining of monthly rental fees for all city-owned properties; (ii) in coordination with the City Legal Officer, facilitate the signing of updated lease contracts between the City government and the tenants; (iii) require the tenants to observe faithful compliance of their duties and obligations by issuing the necessary follow ups/ demand letters to the accountable delinquent tenants/ occupants for the settlement of over-due accounts; (iv) incorporate in the lease contracts the penal provision against delinquent tenants/ occupants, and enforce its implementation, if warranted; and
(b) City Accountant to maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund for the operations of all economic enterprises owned by the City government.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Implemented
CEEMO in coordination with City Treasurer considered ordinance and facilitated lease updates.

(a.ii) Implemented
Updated lease contracts facilitated and follow-ups issued to delinquent tenants.

(a.iii) Not Implemented
Some tenants' monitoring and enforcement measures remain ongoing.

(a.iv) Implemented
Penal provisions incorporated where applicable.

(b) Not Implemented
City Accountant has not yet maintained the Special Accounts in the General Fund for economic enterprises.
observations.reason: Special Accounts in General Fund for economic enterprises not yet set up by City Accountant; tenant monitoring/enforcement ongoing.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Local Chief Executive direct the:
(a) CSWDO to – (i) designate specific personnel who will closely monitor the collection of monthly rentals from the beneficiaries/ tenants; and issue billing and demand letters; and (ii) coordinate with, and seek assistance from the City Legal Officer in communicating with the delinquent tenants to enforce the settlement of their overdue accounts, and to strictly enforce the provisions stipulated in SP Ordinance No. 28, series of 2023; and
(b) City Accountant to review the Masterlist submitted by the CSWDO and effect the necessary adjustment amounting to ₱6,000.00 in the Receivables account.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Implemented
CSWDO designated personnel and improved monitoring; however monitoring gaps remained as payments to Treasurer were not timely communicated to CSWDO.

(a.ii) Not Implemented
Some follow-up legal communications pending; updated lists requested but not received.

(b) Implemented
City Accountant effected necessary adjustment.
observations.reason: Monitoring lapses due to lack of communication between Treasurer and CSWDO; legal follow-ups pending.

observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
GAD mainstreaming efforts continued and compliance improved.

(b) Not Implemented
Revised GPB notification to DILG/Audit Team pending in case of changes.

(c) Implemented
Reporting functions improved and internal data checks instituted.

(d) Implemented
Gender analysis through HGDG administration undertaken.

(e) Implemented
Multi-year GAD Agenda developed using PCW templates.

(f) Implemented
GAD Accomplishment Report timely submitted to Audit Team.
observations.reason: Revised GPB reporting to DILG pending when changes occur; otherwise actions implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Chief Executive
(LCE) instruct:
(a) The City Accountant to (i) effect the necessary adjustment to reflect
the accurate balance of the Advances to SDO account; and (ii)
compute or determine the amount of impairment and provide an
Allowance for Impairment for various dormant Receivable accounts
for fair presentation of the financial statements and as required
under IPSAS 29, using the following adjusting entry:
Account Title Debit Credit
Impairment Loss (per account) xxx
Allowance for Impairment (per account) xxx
(b) The City Accountant and the City Legal Officer to (i) comply with
all the conditions and requirements of COA Circular No. 2023-008
for the possible Request for Write-off of the various dormant
Receivable accounts; and (ii) intensify collection measures or
institute appropriate remedies that would require AOs to settle or
liquidate advances immediately;
(c) The AOs to improve further their compliance with the reporting or
liquidation of CAs; and
(d) The City Accountant and the City Treasurer to refrain from
granting additional CAs to officers and employees unless the
previous CAs given to them are first settled or properly accounted
for.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) Inventory Committee to exert best efforts to undertake the physical
count of all inventory items of the City or consolidate the physical
count conducted by the various offices, and prepare the RPCI and
reconcile the same with the accounting records;
(b) CGSO to consolidate on a weekly basis inventories already
distributed as per the records maintained by the different offices, in
lieu of the RIS, prepare the SSMI and submit the same to the City
Accountant as basis for adjustment of the inventory accounts; and
(c) City Accountant to strictly follow the procedures in recording the
receipts and issuances of inventories using the Perpetual Inventory
Method.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the CGSO
and City Accountant to strictly comply with the required maintenance of
complete and updated forms, registries and reports pertaining to semi-
expendable properties.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the City
Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books to accrue the
accomplished portion of the ongoing projects under the CIP account for
the fair presentation of balances in the financial statements.

We also recommended that the City Engineer and City Planning and
Development Officer (CPDO) continue to furnish the City Accountant with
the Status of Implementation of Infrastructure Projects, Inspection Report
and Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to facilitate the recording of
the accrual of the accomplished portion of ongoing projects and the
corresponding transfer of completed projects to the appropriate asset
accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the:
(a) Inventory Committee and CGSO to (i) conduct complete physical
count of all PPE of the City on or before January 31 of each year,
conclude said inventory count and immediately submit the Report
on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the Audit Team; and
(ii) reconcile the inventory report with the accounting records;
(b) City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjustments in the books
to (i) derecognize the cost of disposed PPE amounting to
₱958,225.00; (ii) correct the overstated depreciation expense for
PPEs acquired in CY 2024 of ₱1,604,658.27; and (iii) provide
depreciation expense for PPEs acquired in prior years- ₱292,025.10,
with the following adjusting entries:
Account Title Debit/ Credit
FOR DISPOSED PROPERTIES STILL RECORDED IN THE BOOKS
Accumulated Depreciation- Other Transportation Equipment 119,644.58
Accumulated Depreciation- Buildings 215,333.34
Accumulated Depreciation- Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment 83,600.00
Accumulated Depreciation- Other Machinery and Equipment 311,402.50
Cash in Bank (amount sold)
Prior Year Adjustment (if any, for gain )
Other Transportation Equipment 127,000.00
Buildings 400,000.00
Disaster Response and Rescue Equipment 88,000.00
Other Machinery and Equipment 343,225.00
FOR FAILURE TO PRO-RATE THE DEPRECIATION EXPENSE
Accumulated Depreciation- Road Networks 390,674.71
Accumulated Depreciation- Other Structures 1,230,070.24
Prior Year Adjustment 1,604,658.27
Accumulated Depreciation- Furniture and Fixtures 16,086.67
FOR NON-PROVISION OF DEPRECIATION EXPENSE
Prior Year Adjustment 292,025.13
Accumulated Depreciation- Other Machineries and Equipment 257,545.25
Accumulated Depreciation- Power Supply System 34,479.88
(c) City Accountant in coordination with the CGSO to (i) complete the
necessary procedures enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 on
the non-existing or missing PPE items costing ₱1,123,650.00; and
(ii) consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to
render a reliable PPE information; and
(d) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining
unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines
and procedures.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE:
(a) instruct the City Accountant to (i) properly monitor and initiate
thorough review of the composition of the Due to NGAs account and
trace the pertinent records and documents or MOA on the fund
transfers from various NGAs; and (ii) effect the necessary
adjustments or corrections, if any, to reflect the correct balances of
the affected accounts in the books; and
(b) remind the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to cause the
immediate return of the unutilized and/ or non- moving funds to the
respective SAs or the BTr in observance of Section 4.9 of COA
Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the City
Accountant and the City Treasurer to cause the immediate remittance of
₱399,504.17 due to the various Barangays and the PGNE to comply with
the provisions of R.A. No. 7160.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the:
(a) CHRMO to furnish the City Accountant with the Summary of Leave
Credits Earned of all elected and regular employees that will be the
basis of the latter in recording in the books of accounts; and
(b) City Accountant to compute the total money value of leave credits
earned and recognize it in the books by debiting Prior Period
Adjustment and/or Terminal Leave Benefits and crediting Leave
Benefits Payable, in accordance with IPSAS 39 and COA Circular
No. 2024-009.

We commended the City Government for its exemplary efforts in
exceeding its target revenue collection on business taxes in pursuit of its
objectives of providing essential social services for the welfare of its
constituents.

However, for the deficiencies noted, we recommended that the LCE
direct the OIC-CBPLO and the City Treasurer to:
(a) coordinate with the Department of Information and
Communication Technology to enhance the BPLS reports module
by incorporating the taxpayers’ subsidiary ledgers for Business
Tax Accounts;
(b) consider updating the Revised Revenue Code to incorporate
stronger provisions on tax collection, including explicit penalties
for non-compliance, mechanisms for escalating cases, and
coordination procedures with the BIR;
(c) establish stricter monitoring and enforcement measures for
businesses that fail to submit the required documents;
(d) coordinate with the BIR to validate financial data from franchise
operations;
(e) ensure full compliance with the Department of Finance (DOF)
guidelines by adopting the prescribed forms and procedures for the
examination of taxpayers' books of accounts; and
(f) establish clear and uniform guidelines on the granting of
condonation of interests and penalties.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the City
Treasurer to (a) continue enhancing collection efforts through a more
aggressive taxpayer engagement and expanding digital payment options;
and (b) coordinate with the City Legal Officer to strictly enforce legal
remedies in accordance with Section 258 of R.A. No. 7160, particularly the
issuance of warrants of levy against delinquent properties.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the City
Engineer and the Local Development Council to (a) properly plan and
schedule all procurement activities pertaining to development projects; (b)
regularly monitor the status and time frame of project implementation and
completion, respectively; and (c) ensure that the use of the 20% DF is
maximized and complies with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 7160 and
its Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR).

12.20. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the (a)
City Accountant and CDRRMO to submit the lacking documentary
requirements; and (b) City Accountant to (i) strengthen her review
function on the requisite documentation of all transactions; and (ii) to
maintain separate RAAOs for LDDRMF.

13.12. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the GFP
to further improve its compliance on GAD regulations by: (a) vigilantly
overseeing the execution of GAD PPAs to ensure their efficient and
effective completion within the approved budget and specified timeframe;
and (b) conforming to the guidelines for reporting changes in the GPB to
the DILG.

14.4. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the CLSB
to (a) properly monitor the implementation of all PPAs and ensure that the
total budgeted amount for SEF projects are implemented or utilized for the
efficient and timely delivery of targeted services and benefits to the
constituents; (b) identify the PPAs that need to be prioritized every year to
avoid the frequent realignment of funds; and (c) coordinate with the City
Accountant, CPDO and City Budget Office in maintaining a monitoring
record of the implementation of PPAs and utilization of the SEF.

15.8. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the
CLSB to (a) refrain from using the SEF for purposes other than those
enumerated in DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017; and
(b) present the specific PPAs to be implemented in the SEF Budget.

15.9. We also recommended that the SP scrutinize the SEF Budget by requiring
the presentation of specific activities to be implemented in every line item
of PPAs stated in general terms, before their approval.

16.30. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
(a) City Accountant and the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to (i)
submit the lacking documentary requirements in compliance with
R.A. No. 9184 and its IRR; (ii) ensure that all succeeding
infrastructure projects has approved CSHPs by the DOLE; (iii)
submit a written agreement, if any, between the City and RD Talens
Construction and Trading of the change in contract duration from
150 days to 240 days for the construction of Phase 3;
(b) City Accountant and City Engineer to (i) review and revisit the
documentation of time extension and work suspension, and if
warranted, impose liquidated damages for the delay in completion of
Phase 3; and
(c) City Accountant to adjust the overstatement of tax withheld if not
yet remitted to the BIR. If already remitted, offset the amount with
the next remittance and pay to the contractor the balance due to him.

16.31. We further recommended that the LCE consider the forfeiture of the
retention money withheld from the payment to the contractor of Phase 1
due to the non-fulfillment of its obligation with the City, and appropriate
the fund to other purposes or projects.

17.16. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE instruct the:
(a) City Accountant and City Treasurer to strategize effective measures
on how to minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays in the
submission of accounts;
(b) City Accountant to (i) require other officials concerned in the
processing and arrangement of DVs with deficiencies to effect
prompt submission thereof to her office; and (ii) ensure the
submission to the Audit Team of the paid DVs and supporting
documents, Trial Balances, and BRSs within the prescribed period;
and
(c) City Budget Officer to promptly submit the monthly SAOBs to the
Audit Team.

18.13. We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE direct the City
Treasurer and the City Accountant to continue the practice of proper
withholding and timely remittance of the full amounts due to the BIR,
GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the –
(a) City Accountant to cause thorough review of accounting and related records and make proper coordination to the City Engineer and CPDO to determine: (i) the composition of the carry-over balances in the CIP accounts totaling ₱13,136,914.82 and (ii) status of the completed projects in prior years totaling ₱84,405,549.08 which remained recognized under the CIP account and effect the necessary corrections/ adjustments/ transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and
(b) City Engineer and CPDO to submit the Status Report of Completion, Inspection Report, Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to the City Accountant to facilitate the recording of the transfer of completed projects to appropriate asset accounts.
observations.managementAction:
(a.i) Implemented
The City Accountant reclassified the completed infrastructure projects from the CIP account to the appropriate asset accounts.

(b) Implemented
The City Engineer submits the documents to the City Accountant.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accountant to:
(a) comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and remittance of withholding taxes on compensation and value added taxes, percentage taxes & expanded taxes from government money payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes that can be used in the implementation of various programs and projects;
(b) require the BIR to refund the over remittance of taxes and make the necessary correcting entry on the previously recorded Other Prepayment account; and
(c) effect the recording in the books the “Due to BIR - General Fund” of ₱800,756.68 for the amount of withholding taxes on compensation pertaining to December 2023 and re-state the correct balance of the account as of December 31, 2023.
observations.managementAction:
(a) Implemented
City complied with BIR rules and regulations and remitted taxes accordingly.

(b) Implemented
Required refund actions with BIR initiated where applicable.

(c) Implemented
Accounting adjustments for ‘Due to BIR - General Fund’ recorded.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to constantly reconcile and work together to effect the full remittance of the previous years’ balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth and consistently adhere with the timely remittances of the total amounts withheld in future periods.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
City Accountant and City Treasurer reconciled and worked together to effect remittances and continued compliance with timely remittances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive continue to adhere with the guidelines set forth in the COA, DBM, DILG, GC for GOCCs, DND Joint Circular No. 2015-01 to ensure safety, peace and order in the City of Palayan.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
The Local Chief Executive and implementing offices continued to observe the guidelines on CAs and monitoring.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local School Board to:
(a) continue to implement the PPAs programmed for the year so that the supplementary needs of basic education will be fully addressed; and
(b) submit the Registry of Qualified Applicants (RQA) to validate the hiring process of the teaching personnel in the LSB, the qualification of the hired teaching personnel, and the validity of the claims/charges of their salaries to SEF.
observations.managementAction:
Implemented
17 out of 23 PPAs were implemented during CY 2024. The CLSB conducted monthly meetings and committed to identify prioritized PPAs at budgeting stage and to properly monitor implementation of PPAs.

(b) Implemented
RQA submission processes improved where applicable.
observations.reason: Frequent realignments occurred during the year due to urgent needs, but overall SEF utilization and implementation improved.

observations.recommendation:

(a) regularly conduct examination on the books of accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/ personnel who are equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the examination of books of accounts as provided under Section 171 of RA7160 to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales /receipts used in the computation of business taxes paid; and
(b) monitor if businesses are engaging on transactions outside their registered line of business and impose different tax rates for such transactions (if any).

The CTO and BPLD have sampled ten establishments for examination and uncovered deficiencies which resulted in collection of deficient taxes for some taxpayers.

(b) Implemented
Monitoring continued and follow-up actions undertaken.

observations.recommendation:
1.13. We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation and Management
agreed to require the (a) accountable officers to improve further their
compliance on the reporting/liquidation of CAs; (b) City Accountant, in
collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to intensify collection measures
or institute appropriate remedy that would require accountable officers to
settle or liquidate immediately their past due accounts; and (c) if conditions
and circumstances on writing-off the accounts are met, direct the City
Accountant and the Legal Officer to initiate the procedures for filing a
request for authority to write-off dormant accounts under COA Circular
No. 2016-005. Moreover, the existing regulations on CAs should be
observed at all times to avoid the state of dormancy.

2.13. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the:
(a) City Accountant to coordinate with the City Treasurer to reconcile
their records of RPT/ SET Receivables and thereafter, conduct regular
reconciliation exercise to support the integrity of the accounts; and
(b) City Treasurer to (i) introduce collection strategy/ies that would
significantly help increase the RPT/SET collection of the City; and
(ii) institute remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the
collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the Local
Government Code of 1991.

3.10. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the (a) Inventory
Committee in coordination with CGSO to exert best effort to complete the
physical count of all inventory items of the City Government, prepare the
RPCSP and reconcile the same with the accounting records; and (b) CGSO
to strictly comply with the required maintenance of complete and updated
forms, registries and reports pertaining to inventories.

4.23. For the deficiencies discussed above, we recommended and Management
agreed to require and instruct the:
(a) Inventory Committee and CGSO to (i) conduct complete physical
inventory of all PPE of the City on or before January 31 of each
year, conclude said inventory count for CY 2023 and immediately
submit the Report on the Physical Count of PPE (RPCPPE) to the
Audit Team; and (ii) reconcile the inventory report with the
accounting records;
(b) City Accountant and CGSO to (i) complete the necessary
procedures enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the
missing/unlocated PPE items and unidentifiable balances; and (ii)
consider the guidelines on one-time cleansing of PPE accounts to
render a reliable PPE information; and
(c) Disposal Committee to facilitate the disposal of the remaining
unserviceable PPEs in accordance with the prescribed guidelines
and procedures.

5.8. In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to
instruct the CGSO to insure all insurable properties of the City with the
GSIS and to coordinate with the City Accountant and the City Treasurer
for the timely processing and payment of GSIS insurance premiums in
compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656.

6.12. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the –
(a) City Accountant to cause thorough review of accounting and related
records and make proper coordination to the City Engineer and CPDO
to determine (i) the composition of the forwarded balances in the CIP
accounts totaling ₱13,136,914.82 and (ii) status of the completed
projects in prior years totaling ₱84,405,549.08 which remained
recognized under the CIP account and effect the necessary
corrections/adjustments/transfer to the appropriate asset accounts for
fair presentation of balances in the financial statements; and
(b) City Engineer and CPDO to submit the Status Report of Completion,
Inspection Report, Certificate of Completion and Acceptance to the City
Accountant to facilitate the recording of the transfer of completed
projects to appropriate asset accounts.

7.12. In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to:
(a) instruct the City Accountant to (i) properly monitor and initiate thorough
review of the composition of the Due to NGAs account and trace the
pertinent records and documents/ MOA on the fund transfers from various
NGAs; and (ii) effect the necessary adjustments/corrections, if any, to
reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts in the books of the
agency; and
(b) remind the City Accountant and the City Treasurer to (i) cause the
immediate return of the unutilized and/ or non- moving funds to the
respective SAs in observance of Section 4.9 of COA Circular No. 94-013
dated December 13, 1994; and (ii) consider the adoption of COA Circular
No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016 on requesting for authority to
write-off of dormant accounts to clear the books of any unaccounted
balances.

8.19. In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to
direct the City Accountant to:
(a) comply with the BIR rules and regulations on the withholding and
remittance of withholding taxes on compensation and value added
taxes, percentage taxes & expanded taxes from government money
payments to assure that the government was not deprived of taxes
that can be used in the implementation of various programs and
projects;
(b) require the BIR to refund the over remittance of taxes and make the
necessary correcting entry on the previously recorded Other
Prepayment account;
(c) effect the recording in the books the “Due to BIR - General Fund” of
₱800,756.68 for the amount of withholding taxes on compensation
pertaining to December 2023 and re-state the correct balance of the
account as of December 31, 2023.

9.8. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the City Accountant
and the City Treasurer to constantly reconcile and work together to effect
the full remittance of the previous years’ balances of Due to GSIS, Pag-
IBIG and PhilHealth and consistently adhere with the timely remittances
of the total amounts withheld in future periods.

10.16. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
(a) CDRRMO in coordination with the CPDO (i) identify and incorporate under a
separate caption in the LDRRMFIP, the specific PPAs to be implemented and
charged against the unexpended prior years’ LDRRMF; and (ii) carefully
monitor the implementation of PPAs for LDRRMF; and
(b) City Accountant to trace the prior year transactions and effect the necessary
adjustment of ₱5,295,681.80 to reflect the correct balance of the Trust
Liabilities-DRRM account as at year-end.

11.10. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Engineer
and the Local Development Council to (a) consider addressing possible
problems on projects proposed for funding under the 20% DF during the
planning phase to avoid delays during the implementation period; (b)
ensure that the three unimplemented projects with total appropriation of
₱10,000,000.00 will be completed as planned; and (c) maximize project
implementation and utilization of the 20% DF in conformity with the
pertinent provisions of RA No. 7160 and its IRR.

12.9. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the City Accountant
to (a) require other officials concerned in the processing and arrangement
of DVs with deficiencies to effect prompt submission thereof to her office;
and (b) ensure the submission to the Audit Team of the paid DVs, Trial
Balances, Financial Statements with supporting documents, and Bank
Reconciliation Statements within the prescribed period.

13.12. We recommended and Management agreed to continue to adhere with the
guidelines set forth in the COA, DBM, DILG, GC for GOCCs, DND Joint
Circular No. 2015-01 to ensure safety, peace and order in the City of
Palayan.

14.8. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the LSB to:
(a) continue to implement the PPAs programmed for the year so that the
supplementary needs of basic education will be fully addressed; and
(b) submit the Registry of Qualified Applicants (RQA) to validate the hiring
process of the teaching personnel in the LSB, the qualification of the
hired teaching personnel, and the validity of the claims/charges of their
salaries to SEF.

15.14. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the CSWMB to (a)
continue to implement and enforce meaningful and relevant PPAs that will
help promote the proper waste management to protect the environment
and the health and safety of the population; (b) strictly enforce the at-source
segregation of wastes thru the “No Segregation, No Collection Policy”; and
(c) revisit the contract agreement with MCWMC including the provisions
on the guaranteed minimum volume of waste per bins and make
clarification on the policy implemented by the MCWMC that is not
contained in the terms and conditions of the Contract.

16.12. In view of the foregoing, we recommended and Management agreed to
instruct the City Treasurer to (a) regularly conduct examination on the
books of accounts of business establishments by assigning staff/personnel
who are equipped with the necessary knowledge and training in the
examination of books of accounts as provided under Section 171 of RA No.
7160 to determine the truthfulness of the declared gross sales/receipts used
in the computation of business taxes paid; and (b) monitor if businesses are
engaging on transactions outside their registered line of business and
impose different tax rates for such transactions (if any).

17.11. We recommended and Management agreed to instruct the CBPLO to (a)
strictly enforce the submission of Audited FS or in its absence, the Sworn
Statement of Gross Receipts/Sales or a copy of ITR from taxpayers to
verify or assess the correct tax base to be used, as provided by 20.04 (d) of
the Revenue Code; (b) monitor the registration/ renewal of Mayor’s Permit
of every business establishment in the City of Palayan; and (c) make proper
coordination with the Sangguniang Panlungsod to amend/ include in the
Revenue Code the imposition of penalties/ surcharges for non-
registration/renewal of Mayor’s Permit.

18.29. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
(a) CEEMO
i. in coordination with the City Treasurer, to consider the proposal of an
ordinance to the Sangguniang Panlungsod covering the imposition or
fining of monthly rental fees for all city-owned properties;
ii. in coordination with the City Legal Officer, facilitate the signing of
updated lease contracts between the City government and the
tenants;
iii. require the tenants to observe faithful compliance of their duties and
obligations by issuing the necessary follow ups/demand letters to the
accountable delinquent tenants /occupants for the settlement of over-
due accounts;
iv. incorporate in the lease contracts the penal provision against
delinquent tenants/ occupants, and enforce its implementation, if
warranted; and
(b) City Accountant
i. to maintain the Special Accounts in the General Fund for the
operations of all economic enterprises owned by the City
government.

19.11. We recommended and Management agreed to direct the:
(a) CSWDO to
i. designate specific personnel who will closely monitor the
collection of monthly rentals from the beneficiaries/tenants;
and issue billing and demand letters; and
ii. coordinate with, and seek assistance from the City Legal
Officer in communicating with the delinquent tenants to
enforce the settlement of their overdue accounts, and to strictly
enforce the provisions stipulated in SP Ordinance No. 28,
series of 2023; and
(b) City Accountant
i. to review the Masterlist submitted by the CSWDO and effect
the necessary adjustment amounting to ₱6,000.00 in the
Receivables account.

20.21. We recommended and Management agreed to require the GAD Focal
Point System to:
(a) continue its efforts to improve gender mainstreaming to achieve full
compliance with the provisions of relevant laws and regulations;
(b) inform/ submit the revised GPB to the DILG and the Audit Team
should there be any update/ changes in the original GPB;
(c) improve the reporting functions and ensure that GAD internal data
are consistent and corroborative by checking the accuracy of the
amounts in order to be useful to the users;
(d) direct all members of the GAD Focal Point System to conduct gender
analysis of the City’s program, project and activities through the
administration of HGDG for purposes of GAD budget attribution;
(e) create a multi-year GAD Agenda as basis for the annual GPB by
using the templates provided in PCW Memorandum Circular No.
2018-04 dated September 19, 2018; and
(f) timely submit to the Audit Team the GAD Accomplishment Report
in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed to
require the:
(a) AOs to improve further their compliance on the reporting/liquidation
of CAs;
(b) City Accountant, in collaboration with the City Legal Officer, to
intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that
would require AOs to settle or liquidate immediately their past due
accounts; and
(c) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts would be
present, direct the City Accountant and the City Legal Officer to
initiate the procedures for the application of the request under COA
Circular 2016-005.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the:
(a) City Treasurer to:
i. introduce collection strategies that would significantly help
increase the RPT/SET collection of the City; and institute
remedies, administrative and/ or judicial action, for the
collection of over-due taxes as provided in Section 256 of the
Local Government Code of 1991;
ii. timely prepare an updated Certified List of Taxpayers and Tax
Due, bearing the names, particulars of the property, location,
amount due and collectible for the applicable year and other
pertinent information; and submit the same to the City
Accountant as the basis in setting up of RPT/SET tax receivable
in the books of accounts of the City at the beginning of the year;
(b) City Accountant to:
i. observe the provision under Section 20 of the New Government
Accounting (NGAS) Manual by using the Certified List of
Taxpayers and Tax Due prepared by the City Treasurer as the
basis in recording the RPT/SET receivables at the beginning of
the year; and
ii. coordinate with the City Treasurer, reconcile their records of
RPT/ SET Receivables and thereafter, conduct regular
reconciliation exercise to support the integrity of the accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:
a) CEEMO
i. in coordination with the City Treasurer, to recommend to the
SP to pass an ordinance covering the imposition or fixing of
monthly rental fees for all city-owned properties;
ii. to issue the necessary follow up/ demand letters to the
accountable delinquent tenants /occupants for the settlement of
over-due accounts or to initiate legal action, if warranted, to
collect the long overdue arrearages from the tenants/
occupants;
iii. to require the tenants to observe faithful compliance of the
duties and obligations, and penalize them in case of any breach
or violation stipulated thereto;
iv. to submit an updated master list of tenants and reconcile the
same with the actual occupants and numbers of market stalls
appearing in the structure layout plan as prepared by the CEO;and
v. in coordination with the City Legal Officer to amend the lease
contracts to include protective clauses and available remedies
of the City against delinquent tenants; and
b) City Accountant
i. to maintain the Special Accounts for operations of the
economic enterprise in the General Fund and recognize in the
book the accrued receivables for rental fees of ₱6,157,261.00; and
ii. to coordinate with CEEMO on accounting and controlling the
accounts relevant to rental and lease contracts.

We recommended and management agreed to:
(a) enjoin the City Accountant and Acting CGSO to (i) maintain complete
and updated property records, settle the established variance in the
property records and reconcile them periodically;
(b) instruct the City Accountant to (i) reconstruct the records of Land
account to prove the validity of recognizing the true untitled lots in the
books; (ii) submit the lapsing schedule to substantiate the depreciation
accounts; and (iii) comply with instructions of COA Circular 2015-008
respecting the preparation and maintenance of records for
infrastructure projects; and
(c) instruct the Acting CGSO to undertake disposal of unserviceable fixed
assets to prevent further deterioration and decline in their value and
to generate additional income from their sale.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the:
(a) City Budget Officer to include in the appropriation sufficient amount
for the insurance coverage of all insurable properties of the City; and
(b) Acting CGSO (i) to insure all insurable properties of the City with the
GSIS and (ii) to coordinate with the City Accountant and the City
Treasurer for the timely processing and payment of GSIS insurance
in compliance with the provisions of RA No. 656.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Bids and Awards
Committee (BAC), BAC Secretariat, Technical Working Group (TWG), and
City Engineering Office to be prudent in exercising their functions and ensure
compliance with the provisions of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR in all
infrastructure procurement and implementation activities, particularly on:
(a) settlement and clearance of right-of-way issue for any infrastructure
project;
(b) proper documentation of works suspension and resumption; and
(c) preparation of prescribed format of ABC. The City should resolve the
issue of boundary dispute with Barangay Mapalad and Barangay
Nauson in order to complete the project that has been in delay for
more than 61 days.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:
(a) Local Housing Board to amend the policy and guidelines on housing
program in order to comprehensively capture and outline the rights and
responsibilities of each party, as well as the assignment of costs, defining
the benefits, and sanctions for default and breach leading to contract
termination;
(b) CPDO and the City Engineer to closely monitor the implementation of the
program and impose, if warranted, liquidating damages against the
contractors for the noted delays in the execution of the project;
(c) CSWDO to (i) closely monitor the collection of monthly rentals from the
beneficiaries; (ii) establish the correct amount of collectibles from them
and report the same to the City Treasurer for collection and City
Accountant for recording; and
(d) City Accountant to (i) submit the lacking documents above-stated; and
(iii) effect the necessary adjustment to accrue the uncollected rental fees.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the:
(a) CHO to continue to (i) exert best efforts in implementing its COVID-
19 Vaccination Program and intensify the information campaign to
the public to spread awareness of COVID-19 vaccine benefits to
achieve the Herd Immunity as a way to fight the said virus; and (ii)
render an Inventory Report to the CAO on the receipt, issuance and
balances of vaccines and other inventories directly received from
outside sources for recording and disclosure purposes; and
(b) henceforth, City Accountant to disclose in the Notes to Financial
Statements the donated vaccines to the City based on the Inventory
Report submitted by the CHO.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the CPDO, CSWDO,
CDRRMO and City Engineer to continuously observe the time frame of
scheduled implementation/completion of the projects to ensure efficient
delivery of public services to the intended beneficiaries.

On LDRRMF disbursements, we recommended and management agreed to
instruct the CDRRMO to observe strictly the rules on utilization of LDRRMF
as stated in DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 dated March 25, 2013 and ensure
that only eligible programs/projects/activities are to be charged against the
LDRRMF.

We recommended and management agreed to reconstitute the GFPS which
will take a lead role in direction-setting, advocacy, planning, monitoring and
evaluation, and technical advisory in mainstreaming GAD perspectives in the
LGU PPAs and the GFPS to:
(a) continue to (i) develop and implement more GAD related PPAs that
advocate women empowerment, promote gender equality and
improve the welfare and protection of rights of women; and (ii) strictly
adhere to PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC Nos. 2013-01 in the
implementation of planned projects and maximum utilization of
appropriated funds for the purpose;
(b) administer gender analysis through the Harmonized Gender and
Development Guidelines (HGDG) in the attribution of costs of regular
program/project to GAD; and
(c) institutionalize an internal review mechanism for a consistent, reliable
and relevant reporting of GAD operations.

We recommended and the LCE being the Chairperson of the LSB agreed to:
(a) ensure that all SEF disbursements are supported with complete
documentation and the execution of PPAs are in accordance with the
guidelines and parameters prescribed by the JC; and
(b) conduct the proper monitoring with respect to efficient execution and
maximum utilization of the annual SEF budget and with the view to
accomplishing fully the predetermined programs and projects in
furtherance of and in harmony with the national government’s goals
relevant to basic education

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the:
a. Property Officer of the City and the DepEd Schools Division Office to
work closely on establishing an internal policy or agreement on
managing SEF property to ensure that (i) accountability over issued
property items is established at any point in time; (ii) the PAR is
updated every three years or whenever there is a change of
accountability over the item; and (iii) the existence and condition of
the PPE items are properly determined every year.
b. Property Custodian to study the possibility of transferring, by way of
donation, to the DepED School Division, SEF-funded property which
are for use directly in the classroom like teacher’s chairs and tables,
instructional/teaching materials, etc. so that the person in actual
physical possession/control of the SEF property is made responsible
for its proper use and be accountable thereto.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Petty Cash
Custodian to ensure the use of petty cash vouchers for payments out of the
PCF.

We recommended that the LCE require the AOs stationed at CSWDO to
apply for the increase of the amount of bond based on their respective cash
accountabilities and applicable rates provided in the Revised Schedule of
Premium Rates per Treasury Circular No. 02-2009 on fidelity bond.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation and management agreed to
direct the City Treasurer and City Accountant to exert extra efforts and
improve their compliance on reports submission to curtail the incurrence of
delays.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the City Accountant to
ensure the prompt preparation and submission of the BRS to the audit team
within the required period for timely review and verification of the monthly
reported cash in bank balances.

We recommended and management agreed to direct the City Accountant to:
(a) remain compliant with the rules and regulations and continuously remit
in full the applicable dues within the prescribed period; and
(b) resolve the issues of unreconciled balances.

for the application of the request
under COA Circular 2016-005.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) accountable officers improve further their compliance on the reporting/liquidation of cash advances; (b) City Accountant, in collaboration with the City Legal Officer, intensify collection measures or institute appropriate remedy that would require accountable officers to settle or liquidate their past due accounts; and (c) if conditions and circumstances on writing off the accounts would be present, City Accountant and the City Legal officer initiate the procedures for the application of the request under COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that management:
enjoin the City Accountant and CGSO to maintain complete and updated property records, reconcile them periodically and settle the established variance in the property records;
instruct the City Accountant to (i) reconstruct the records of Land account to prove the validity of recognizing the true untitled lots in the books; and (ii) comply with instructions of COA Circular 2015-008 respecting the preparation and maintenance of records for infrastructure projects; and
instruct the CGSO to undertake disposal of unserviceable fixed assets to prevent further deterioration and decline in their value in order to generate additional income from their sale.

We recommended and management agreed to instruct the City Accountant to revisit the prior year transactions and conduct the reconciliation of the balances of Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts and effect the necessary adjustments as warranted to bring their balances into agreement.

We recommended that the (a) City Accountant and City Treasurer (i) cause the immediate return of the unutilized or idle funds to the Bureau of Treasury in observance of COA regulations and DBM guidelines; and (ii) ensure that all incoming trusts are fully documented and provisions on financial reporting are timely complied with; and (b) responsible offices (i) observe prudence in complying with the terms and conditions of the sponsoring agencies in implementing the PPAs; and (ii) make use of the opportunity to utilize the fund for LGSF-DRRAP by proposing a valid PPA prescribed under LBC 134.

We recommended that management (a) continue its efforts to substantially comply with the guidelines set forth by R.A. No. 10121; (b) require the CDRRMO and the City Accountant to comply with the reporting guidelines provided in COA Circular No. 2012-002 by maintaining separate RAAOs for LDRRMF and regularly submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF; and (c) direct the responsible offices to observe prudence in the timely implementation of projects governed by agreements and ensure that the terms and conditions therein stipulated are followed satisfactorily.

We recommended that management continue to observe the guidelines on the 20% development fund and instruct the City Engineer and CPDO to coordinate on the strict monitoring of the status of implementation of all projects and ensure the timely implementation and completion of all PPAs of the 20% development fund for the immediate utilization of the intended beneficiaries.

We also recommended that management require the (a) Bids and Awards Committee; BAC Secretariat, and Technical Working Group to (i) submit the requisite documentary requirements for the above procurements and henceforth, ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of R.A. No. 9184 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 in all procurements through public bidding; and (b) City Accountant to diligently perform her review of the requisite documentation of procurements.

We recommended that the LSB (a) ensure that all SEF disbursements are supported with complete documentation and the execution of PPAs are in accordance with the guidelines and parameters prescribed by the JC; and (b) conduct the proper monitoring with respect to efficient execution and maximum utilization of the annual SEF budget with the end view of accomplishing fully the predetermined programs and projects in furtherance and in harmony with the national government’s goals relevant to basic education.

We recommended and management agreed that the GAD Focal Point System through the GAD Focal Person (a) administer gender analysis through the use of Harmonized Gender and Development Guidelines (HGDG) for the cost of major PPAs to be attributed to GAD; and (b) timely submit to the Audit Team the GPB and AR in compliance with COA Circular No. 2014-001

We recommended that management direct –
The City Treasurer and the City Accountant (i) to strategize and work closely on improving their performance in terms of the submission of the periodic financial accounts and reports to COA and (ii) minimize, if not eliminate the incurrence of delays; and
the City Accountant to transmit immediately to the Office of the Auditor the financial accounts and reports that are due for submission.

We commended management for its continuous efforts to comply with the COVID-19 vaccination program guidelines, a way of providing protection to their constituents from adverse effects of the virus.  We also recommended and management agreed to continue to administer vaccines efficiently so that local herd immunity with booster shot can be attained in the soonest time possible.

We recommended that management direct:
a.) the responsible officers to (i) formulate and document the policy and guidelines on housing program; (ii) closely monitor the implementation of the program and deliver the outputs by utilizing optimally the fund which is set aside through prioritization; and (iii) justify the idleness of the program fund during the entire year;
b) the City Accountant in coordination with the City Engineer to submit the requisite documents above-stated: and
c) the BAC to comply fully with the provisions of GPPB No. 18-2006, particularly on the provision of documentary requirements in the procurement of Pakyaw Labor.

We recommended and management agreed to enhance its compliance on the publicity and notification of City’s PPAs by: (a) submitting within ten days the notification to the Auditor that the appropriate project signboards and/or public notices are already posted in the project site; and (b) requiring the City Engineer to (i) employ close supervision on the implementation of PPAs, and maintain a reliable mechanism on maintaining and updating the monthly reports on PPAs’ accomplishments; and (ii) closely monitor the installation of signboards/tarpaulins by the contractor/supplier and recommend the immediate rectification had there been incomplete details or erroneous inscription.
